title: BEST Discovery Manifest
description: Schema for the /.well-known/best discovery manifest. Every BEST-compliant endpoint must expose this at GET /.well-known/best.

services.description: Service definitions with transport bindings. Keys are fully qualified service names (e.g. io.best.agents).

agents.description: Snapshot of the service descriptors this endpoint hosts (optional). This is a discovery hint, not a live directory — BEST defines no registry endpoint. Implementations that manage services dynamically may expose that as a domain (a list-services query and RegisterService command) under their own namespace. For dynamic systems this array may be omitted or contain only representative examples.

webhook.description: Callback configuration for push event delivery over HTTP.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint to receive events as CloudEvents (HTTP binding).

secret.description: HMAC secret for payload signing. Write-only — accepted on registration, never returned in read responses.

serviceDescriptor.description: The identity card for a BEST-compliant service — what it does, what commands it accepts, what events it produces. Declared in the manifest's agents array.

version.description: Semver version of the service implementation currently running (e.g. '1.2.0'). Allows consumers to know which version is live and supports gradual rollouts.

type.description: Service type classification (e.g. negotiation-service, pricing-engine, sensor).

endpoint.description: Base URL of this service's BEST surface. Consumers append /.well-known/best to discover transport bindings and send commands. Optional — omit if the service is not directly addressable.

accepts.description: CloudEvent type strings (PascalCase) identifying the command types this service ingests.

produces.description: CloudEvent type strings (PascalCase) identifying the event types this service publishes as a result of processing.

metadata.description: Opaque, service-defined key-value configuration. Intended for operational settings such as AI model name, system prompt, or provider credentials. The protocol does not interpret or validate the contents. Clients may read this field; the service owns and manages it.

service.description: A top-level service domain the runtime supports.

capability.description: A composable capability within a service.

name.description: Fully qualified capability name using reverse-domain notation. BEST built-in capabilities use the io.best.* prefix (e.g. io.best.agents.commands). Custom or domain-specific capabilities must use a unique reverse-domain prefix owned by the implementer (e.g. io.dotquant.trading, com.acme.inventory).

spec.description: URL to the capability specification. Required for io.best.* capabilities. Custom capabilities should host their own specification page and link it here when available.

schema.description: URL to the JSON Schema for this capability. Required for io.best.* capabilities. Custom capabilities should host their own schema and link it here when available.

service.description: Key of the service (in the manifest's services object) that implements this capability. Required when the capability name prefix does not match the service name — for example, when a custom service (io.dotquant.trading) implements a standard BEST capability (io.best.agents.commands). Consumers use this field to resolve which http.endpoint to call for the capability's endpoints. Omit only when the capability name shares its prefix with exactly one service key (e.g. io.best.agents.* capabilities under the io.best.agents service).

status.description: Capability availability status. 'active' means fully implemented. 'partial' means a subset of the capability's endpoints or features are implemented — consumers must not assume all required endpoints exist. 'planned' means not yet implemented. Omitted means active.

endpoints.description: Machine-readable list of HTTP endpoints exposed by this capability. Paths are relative to http.endpoint. The HTTP method signals whether each operation is a read (GET) or a write (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE). Consumers can use this to discover the catalogue URL, determine mutability, and self-bootstrap without reading the spec.

method.description: HTTP method. GET is read-only. POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE mutate state.

tenants.description: Multi-tenant manifest discovery. When present, signals that this is a multi-tenant host and provides a URI template for consumers to obtain a tenant-scoped manifest. The tenant manifest returned at the expanded URI is fully self-contained and must not include a tenants block of its own.

manifest.description: RFC 6570 URI template for tenant manifest discovery (e.g. https://api.example.com/.well-known/best/{tenantId}). {tenantId} is the only defined variable. Consumers expand this template with a known tenant ID to obtain a fully-resolved, self-contained manifest.

pushChannels.description: Push channels this capability supports for delivering events to callers. All fields are optional — absence means the channel is not supported. Typically declared on the io.best.agents.events capability.

sse.description: Server-Sent Events stream supported at GET /events/stream — recommended for browser apps, CLI tools, and locally-running agents that cannot expose a public webhook endpoint.

webhook.description: Webhook callback delivery is supported — callers may register via POST /subscriptions.

endpoint.description: Consumer-facing base URL for all HTTP operations. This must be the public or proxy-facing address reachable by consumers — never an internal backend address. All capability endpoint paths are appended to this URL.

authentication.description: Authentication requirements for accessing this BEST endpoint. Consumers must present credentials according to the declared scheme before calling any protected endpoint.

type.description: Authentication mechanism. Use 'none' for public endpoints.

scheme.description: For 'bearer': the Authorization header scheme (e.g. 'Bearer'). For 'apiKey': the header or query parameter name.

in.description: Where the API key is passed when type is 'apiKey'.

tokenUrl.description: Token endpoint URL for OAuth2 or token-based flows.

mcpAuthentication.description: Authentication requirements for connecting to an MCP server. Consumers (including AI agents and IDE tooling) must read this block and supply the declared credentials when connecting.

headers.description: Required HTTP headers for 'apiKey' type. Use when more than one header is needed (e.g. an API key and a tenant ID). For single-header API key auth, a one-entry array is preferred.

tokenUrl.description: Token endpoint URL for 'oauth2' or token-based flows.

mcpAuthHeader.description: Descriptor for a single required HTTP header when connecting to an MCP server.

description.description: Human-readable description of what value to supply.

example.description: Pre-filled example value. Tooling (e.g. VS Code Copilot MCP config generator) may use this to populate the header automatically. A per-tenant manifest may pre-fill this with the resolved tenant ID.
